assignments.title: Assignment To Do List Review And Update
assignments.description_html:
Review & Update your Weekly Assignment To-Do List

NOTE: You should have nothing on your to-do list! If you do have outstanding work, see Arianna and Haniya immediately.

Review your assignments in the gradebook. Make note of assignments you haven't yet submitted, as well as assignments for which you have an "incomplete." You'll need to fix these assignments according to your instructors' feedback as soon as possible.

Document these incomplete or unsubmitted assignments in your Weekly Assignment To-Do List which you've already created on Google Docs.

Assignment completion expectation

It is expected that you will complete assignments that are past due. Please take time over the weekend to do this. You are also invited to attend Focus Friday if you'd like to work with classmates and others on the assignments. Details about Focus Friday and how to join are posted in the class Slack channel each Friday before the start of Block A.

It is suggested that you complete assignments in the following order:

  • Assignments from this week, while the material is fresh in your mind
  • Cannons Coffee assignment, if it is not yet complete. You cannot move on from ITP if this assignment is not marked complete.
  • Older assignments from previous weeks

As always, if you want help with your work or need additional explanations, you should make an office hours appointment to get help with Arianna and/or Haniya.

How to turn this in

Turn in the updated Google Doc to this assignment. Make sure Haniya and Arianna have access to see it.

 

Late assignments/ Incomplete Assignments

In the case of incomplete assignments, please note that it is crucial to provide as much information as possible. This will help us understand why you were unable to meet the deadline and how we can best support you in completing the work.

In the text entry for the assignment, please include the following:

  1. Why the assignment is late
  2. What you still need to do for the assignment
  3. Your expected timeline for completing the work
  4. If you would like to copy and paste the example here as a template please do so

**You must include a link in order for your submission to work. Feel free to use www.google.com.

For example:

 “The ________ project is not complete because _________. I have ________, ________, and ________ complete but still need more time to complete _________, _________, and _________. I estimate that I will have this done by _________________.”

assignments.title: Css Selector Practice
assignments.description_html:
Fork and save this CodePen to your account: https://codepen.io/team/anniecannons/pen/JjQwqvX

Write some CSS styles to achieve these looks. Do not change the HTML.

1. Set all text to display using the font Suse.

2. Set the words "freestone" and "clingstone" to display bold.

3. Set the words "two types" in the paragraph starting on line 5 to display bold and #ff0000 text. The rest of the paragraph on line 5 should have #0000ff text.

4. In the list of the members of the genus Prunus, set every other list item to have a background color of #ffcc00. Do not have any text outside of those list items change color.

5. The link on line 9 should be #B5D741.
The link on line 22 should be #041853.
The link on line 31 should be #E64203.

6. All links should have their underlines disappear on hover. The link color should not change.

assignments.title: Linkedin Profile Review And Action Plan
assignments.description_html:
LinkedIn Profile Review & Action Plan

Task: Put on the hat of your future employer and critically assess your LinkedIn profile. Identify gaps or areas for improvement to ensure you're presenting yourself as an ideal candidate for software engineering roles.

Instructions:

  1. Review Your LinkedIn Profile Through the Lens of an Employer:
    • Headline: Does it immediately convey your career aspirations and demonstrate your expertise in software engineering? Is it compelling enough to catch the attention of recruiters looking for candidates with your skills?
    • Summary: Does it clearly articulate your skills, passion for software engineering, and the value you bring to a team? Is it optimized with relevant keywords to show up in employer searches?
    • Experience: Are your roles described in a way that highlights your technical contributions and achievements? Have you detailed specific tech stacks, tools, and tangible results that would be attractive to a future employer?
    • Skills & Endorsements: Are your most in-demand skills (e.g., JavaScript, React, Node.js) prominently displayed and endorsed? Could you add more endorsements to validate your expertise in critical areas?
    • Projects: Are you showcasing your most relevant GitHub projects that demonstrate real-world skills? Have you linked to these projects in the "Projects" section to allow employers to review your work?
    • Certifications: Do you have certifications that prove your proficiency in software engineering technologies? Could you add any that make you stand out more to an employer?
  2. Create an Employer-Focused List of Improvements: Be specific about the changes you would make to appeal directly to hiring managers. For example:
    • "Add links to key GitHub projects under the 'Projects' section, highlighting full-stack and front-end capabilities."
    • "Rewrite the summary to focus on my proficiency in JavaScript, React, and problem-solving skills in software engineering."
    • "Request additional endorsements for high-demand skills like React, JavaScript, and Node.js from colleagues and mentors."
    • "Update work experience descriptions to emphasize my technical skills, including specific tools and frameworks used in each project."

Deliverable:

Create a LinkedIn Action Plan in a Google Doc. List the specific updates you plan to implement, such as revised summary text, added skills, project links, and requests for endorsements. Make sure these changes align with what your future employer would be looking for in a software engineer.

assignments.title: Online Job Platforms And Networking
assignments.description_html:
Assignment: Exploring Online Job Platforms

Objective:

Your goal is to set up a profile and customize your job search filters on at least two different job boards. This will help you tailor your job search experience and optimize your chances of finding relevant opportunities.

Resources:

Be sure to check out AnnieCannons' [AnnieCannons JOB PLATFORMS ], which includes:

  • Online job platforms

  • Professional networking platforms

  • Additional resources for job seekers

Feel free to make a copy if you'd like to edit or customize it!

Instructions:

  1. Choose at least two job boards to create profiles on and set up customized job filters.

    • Popular options include: Indeed, LinkedIn, Glassdoor, welcometothejungle, OutinTech.

  2. Adjust filters to match your job search preferences, such as:

    • Location (remote/in-office/hybrid)

    • Job type (full-time, part-time, contract, internship)

    • Industry or specific roles

  3. Take a screenshot of your customized job search settings for each platform.

  4. Submit your screenshots below.

Reminder:

Tech communities and forums are excellent ways to engage with like-minded professionals. They offer opportunities to

  • Learn from others

  • Seek guidance on technical topics

  • Stay updated on industry trends and best practices

Consider joining platforms such as

GitHub, Dev.to, Stack Overflow, OutInTech, Women Who Code, Techqueria, Black Tech Pipeline

or relevant Discord/Slack communities to expand your professional network!

assignments.title: Secret Code Exchange
assignments.description_html:
We are playing a secret code exchange game for the winter holidays! To participate, please fill out this form. 

 

How the secret code exchange works

  • Each participant will be randomly matched with a gift recipient. We will secretly tell you your gift recipient once everyone fills out the form. 
  • Once you are matched with someone, you will make a CodePen for them as a gift. Someone else will make you a CodePen too. 
  • You may use any combination of HTML, CSS, and/or Javascript to make your CodePen. 
  • You must submit the link to your CodePen by Thursday, December 5th. 
  • We will do a grand reveal of everyone's CodePen gift during the last week of ITP before class ends on Thursday, December 12th.
  • After we have shared all of the CodePens, you may reveal your Secret Coder identity to your gift recipient. 

 

Your CodePen is due on Thursday, December 5th! Have fun! 🎁

assignments.title: Prompt Engineering Course
assignments.description_html:
Prompt Engineering Course Overview

🧠 Grow with Google—Coursera Course Series

You’ve been invited to join the Grow with Google Learning Program on Coursera—an opportunity to build in-demand skills with Generative AI.

🔑 Getting Started

  1. Check your inbox for the invite to the program.
  2. Accept the invite and notify Dawn once completed.
  3. You will then be added to the course titled
     Generative AI Introduction and Prompting in Coursera on Coursera.

📘 Course Requirements
You’ll see four courses in the series.

  • ✅ Required: Google Prompting Essentials
  • 📌 Optional: The remaining three courses can be completed independently, outside of class.

Estimated time to complete: ~6 hours
Deadline: Complete the required course by Wednesday, June 25, 2025

🎯 Why This Course Matters
As developers, clear and effective use of AI tools is now a core skill. Whether you're debugging, planning content, or analyzing data, knowing how to write strong prompts helps you:

  • Save time
  • Improve accuracy
  • Get better results from AI tools

The course uses Google’s Generative AI, but feel free to apply what you learn using ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, or any other tool of your choice.

📚 What You’ll Learn

  • How to structure prompts using the Task → Context → Evaluate → Iterate framework
  • Techniques for brainstorming, summarizing, rewriting, and formatting
  • Prompting for data analysis, visualizations, and spreadsheet fixes
  • Advanced skills like prompt chaining and prompt versioning

✅ What You’ll Walk Away With

  • A strong foundational framework for prompt writing
  • Tools for working faster and smarter with AI
  • Enhanced communication skills for technical and non-technical tasks
  • A resume-ready skill: Prompt Engineering
  • Confidence using AI in real-world dev tasks

🌟 New: Expanded Coursera Access By accepting your invite, you now have access to additional courses and career certificates within the Grow with Google Coursera program. If you find a course or pathway that interests you, book time with Dawn to talk about how it could align with your career goals.

📤 Submission Requirements
To receive credit for completing the Prompt Engineering course:

  1. Upload a screenshot of your course completion (from Coursera).

  2. Complete the Post-Course Survey here.

Need help or haven’t received your course invite?
 📩 Message Dawn directly via Slack.
 💬 Questions about the course or your progress? Reach out any time!

assignments.title: Cannons Coffee Inquiry Page and Login Page Forms
assignments.description_html:
Complete the prep work needed to start this project.

Part 1: Inquiry page

On the inquiry.html page you created as part of the prep work, do the following:

  1. Create an inquiry form that includes:
    1. Name
    2. Email
    3. Phone number
    4. Comments
    5. Dropdown to choose favorite coffee flavor with at least 3 different flavors
    6. 2 Radio buttons asking if they are interested in buying coffee or seeking employment
    7. Submit button
  2.  When the user clicks Submit: 
    1. The form data will show in the console
    2. A thank you message will show on the webpage using a template literal, that says "Thank you [name]! We appreciate your interest in [buying coffee/seeking employment]. Your favorite coffee flavor is [flavor1/flavor2/flavor3] and your comments are [comments]. We will soon contact you at [email] or [phone number]."
    3. The form will disappear when the message shows on the webpage (BONUS)
  3. Remember that your JavaScript will be written in custom-inquiry.js

Part 2: Login Page

On the login page you created as part of the prep work, do the following:

  1. Create a login form that includes:
    1. Name
    2. Email 
    3. Password
    4. Submit Button
  2. When the user clicks Submit:
    1. The form data will show in the console
    2. A welcome message will show on the webpage using a template literal that says "Welcome, [name]! You are logged in!"
    3. The form will disappear when the message shows on the webpage (BONUS)
  3. Remember that your JavaScript will be written in custom-login.js

Part 3: Push to GitHub

Once your code is complete, go live in VS Code and test your forms. If everything is working as expected, do the following:

  1. Push your code to your existing Cannons Coffee Github repo.
  2. See your result on Github Pages.
  3. As always, use the documentation guides to help you (look for the section "How to push your changes to VS Code").

How to turn this in

Submit the link to your Github repo and the link to your live webpage in Github Pages.

assignments.description_html:

In your Notion space, find your Personal Operating Manual, which you created in TCF.

Copy some or all of the material and paste it in the HTML box.

Now mark it up with HTML. 

You are not required to transfer over all information, but include at least 3 sections of it.

Include at least one image. Here's some choices:

  • https://assets.codepen.io/t-15440/itp-pexels-nietjuh-796602.jpg
  • https://assets.codepen.io/t-15440/itp-pexels-ken-tomita-127057-389818.jpg
  • https://assets.codepen.io/t-15440/itp-pexels-prateekkatyal-2740956.jpg 

Include at least 2 links. Suggestions:

  • Link to a type of software (Zoom, Slack)
  • Link to products (your chair, your computer, your desk)
  • Link to music/play lists, etc.

 

Sample of a POM 

As an example, here is the text from a sample completed Personal Operating Manual. This does not reflect the correct markup.

Name: Jane Doe
Role: Software Engineer
Working Hours: 9 AM - 5 PM (Mon-Fri) (PST)
Best Time for Meetings: 10 AM - 12 PM (PST)

Communication Preferences:

  • Email: Preferred for detailed information, response within 24 hours.
  • Slack: For quick questions, response within 1 hour.
  • Video Calls: For real-time feedback, preferably scheduled.

Feedback Preferences:

  • Receiving Feedback: Via email with specific examples.
  • Giving Feedback: In 1:1 meetings, focusing on specific tasks.

Personal Details:

  • Hobbies & Interests: Hiking, reading, and painting.
  • Caregiving Responsibilities: ex. Primary caregiver for elderly parents.
  • Fun Fact About Me: I’ve visited 10 countries!

Work Environment Preferences:

  • Sensory Preferences: Noise-canceling headphones, ergonomic chair.
  • Accessibility Needs: Screen reader compatibility, adjustable desk height.

Work Priorities:

  • Current Projects: Database optimization.

  • Favorite Quote: “Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it’s bad.”

  • Specific Needs/Preferences: Clear, direct communication, written instructions.

Now that you've spent a full week coding, how do you feel about working with HTML and CSS? Where are you feeling comfortable? Where do you feel confused?

Fork and save the following CodePen to your account: https://codepen.io/team/anniecannons/pen/PoMoYXV

Build the Farmer's Market home page! Here's some things to do:

1. Before you write any code, read through the existing HTML to understand how the page is structured. The HTML is done, and you shouldn't need to change any of the HTML to style this page. You should focus on writing CSS.

white: #ffffff;
cream: #fff0cb;
carrot: #f49619;
rust: #b55232;
ironstone: #824737;
pine: #4c7031;
black: #000000;

Mobile: https://assets.codepen.io/t-15440/itp-fm-mobile.jpg

Desktop (925px and larger): https://assets.codepen.io/t-15440/itp-fm-desktop.jpg

d. Finally, format the footer, which should be pretty quick.

assignments.title: Cannons Coffee: Annotating designs
assignments.description_html:
Refer to the designs found in the Cannons Coffee directory.

There are three designs for the home page and two designs for the latte page. Annotate the desktop design for Home Page and desktop design for Latte Page in detail, then for tablet and mobile, you may only annotate the parts of the design that have changed.

Using Canva or a similar tool, annotate these images. Make notes about anything you should consider, but especially pay attention to the following:

  • Identify where you'll use Flexbox or Grid to create layouts
    • Include what changes are made over responsive design by identifying how many columns and rows are in each screen size.
  • Identify types of content/structure you have in the document (navbar, cards, header, footer, links as buttons, images, headings, etc)
  • Find CodePens you have coded previously that match up with patterns you see in these designs? You do not need to find a previous CodePen for each pattern, a few that can help you to refer back to are fine!
    • Example: Does the navbar match one you coded previously?
    • Example: Do you have a CodePen with cards that match what you see here?
    • Record those CodePen URLs on your annotation document with notes about what matches and what you might need to change (colors, media query breakpoints, etc).

Note: This assignment must be turned in on Wednesday at the end of Block B. Your Instructors will review to make sure your annotation looks good before you move into the task list.

If you finish annotations before the end of Block B on Wednesday, please Slack/Zoom chat your Instructors, so they can review and not be a blocker to your progress.

****Please take your time to complete the Apprenti Application using this INIQUE link THAT IS IN THE QR CODE ON THE PDF

Application Process

  • Eligibility:
    • Must be 18+ years of age, have a high school diploma, and be authorized to work in the U.S.
    • Applicants are required to take assessments measuring math, logic, critical thinking, and emotional intelligence.
  • Assessment:
    • Assessment scores (80% or above) are used to determine eligibility for the apprenticeship pool. These are not timed.
    • Each applicant has four attempts to complete the assessment, with the highest score in each section being used.
  • Matching with Employers:
    • Apprenti matches applicants with job opportunities based on their geographical and occupational preferences.
  • Behavioral Interviews:
    • Interviews focus on understanding candidates' motivations and readiness for the apprenticeship, even if they have no prior tech experience.

Check out the resources for preparing for the assessment
Once you are successfully onboarded, send Dawn a direct message on Slack.

assignments.title: Onboarding Students to Indeed
assignments.description_html:
Onboarding Students to Indeed 

Objective:

By the end of this assignment, you will have successfully created a robust Indeed profile, integrated your alternative education experiences (boot camps, apprenticeships) using tools like Skill Connect, and gained practical insights into navigating Indeed to find relevant job opportunities. This will set the foundation for your job search, networking, and professional growth.

Steps for You:

1. Leverage Skill Connect to Highlight AnnieCannons and Alternative Education

Skill Connect is a tool that helps you showcase non-traditional education on your resume. If you’ve completed boot camps or apprenticeships, this is a vital resource for reflecting those experiences.

  • Action Step: Create a Skill Connect account to begin building a resume that highlights your hands-on projects and learning outcomes from boot camps and other alternative education programs.
  • Get Started: [Access Skill Connect here].

2. Build a Complete and Engaging Indeed Profile

Having a well-rounded Indeed profile increases your visibility to recruiters and future employers.
Follow these steps:

  • Sign Up: Visit Indeed to create an account if you don't already have one.
  • Complete Your Profile: Add key details such as work history, skills, and education. Be sure to include all alternative education experiences, like coding boot camps, apprenticeships, and certifications.
  • Professional Summary: Write a brief but impactful summary showcasing your unique strengths and value. Tailor this to emphasize your specialized training (e.g., coding, data analysis) and real-world projects you’ve completed.
  • Create or Upload Your Resume: Upload your resume from Skill Connect. Ensure it includes relevant keywords for better visibility in searches by recruiters.

3. Integrate Your Skill Connect Resume into Your Indeed Profile

Once your Skill Connect resume is built:

  • Download or copy your Skill Connect resume.
  • Revise your resume to the role that you are eeking - ex., front-end  developer, junior developer
  • Upload it to Indeed or manually enter your education and skills into your profile.
  • Tip: Use clear, descriptive language for non-traditional learning experiences, like:
    “Completed a full-stack development boot camp focusing on HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and React.”
    Also, list any specific skills learned during apprenticeships or projects (e.g., Agile methodologies, client work, etc.).

4. Submit Your Resume Draft

Once you’ve completed the first draft of your resume on Skill Connect, download it and then upload it it below. This will allow for review and feedback on your progress.

assignments.title: Set up Job Filter in Indeed
assignments.description_html:
Steps for Using the Indeed Job Search Section
*remember you can set up multiple job alerts

Now that you have set up your job profile, you are ready to begin the job search and set the filters 🤗

  1. Use Filters to Customize Your Job Search

    • Click on the search bar at the top of the page.

    • Enter "What" – Type in job titles, keywords, or company names that match your skills.

    • Enter "Where" – Specify the city, state, or remote options based on your preferences.

    • Click "Find Jobs" to see the results.

  2. Refine Your Search Further

    • Use filters like:

      • Salary estimate (e.g., $40,000+, $60,000+)

      • Job type (Full-time, Part-time, Contract, Internship, etc.)

      • Company name to search for jobs from specific employers

      • Experience level (Entry-level, Mid-level, Senior, etc.)

      • Date posted (Last 24 hours, Last 7 days, etc.)

    • Click "Apply Filters" to update your job list.

  3. Set Up Job Alerts

    • After setting your search filters, look for the "Get Job Alerts" button.

    • Click it and enter your email to receive updates on new job postings that match your criteria.

    • Adjust your alert frequency in settings (Daily, Weekly, etc.).

  4. Apply and Track Applications

    • Click on job listings to read descriptions and requirements.

    • Click "Apply Now" for direct applications or "Apply on Company Site" if needed.

    • Save jobs to apply later using the "Save Job" button.

    • Check your Indeed dashboard to track applications and responses.

          • Submit a screenshot of the Job Alert Below

assignments.title: 📋 Watch Getting a Software Engineering Job, v3 Frontend Masters &  Create a to-do list
assignments.description_html:
📝 Overview

This assignment helps you kick off your job search with the right strategies, mindset, and tools.
You’ll learn how to optimize your resume, GitHub, LinkedIn, and prepare for technical interviews — and create your own actionable job search roadmap.

By the end, you’ll have a clear plan and a baseline to start improving your professional materials.

🎯 Objectives

  • Learn best practices for landing a software engineering job.

  • Assess your current professional materials (resume, GitHub, LinkedIn).

  • Create a structured To-Do List for your job search.

  • Book a follow-up session to discuss next steps.

🪜 Instructions

1. Watch the Course : Getting a Software Engineering Job, v3 Frontend Masters

📺 Watch the recorded course:
Getting a Software Engineering Job, v3 – Frontend Masters
"Software Engineering Job Search Success: Resume, GitHub, LinkedIn & Technical Interviews"
by Jerome Hardaway.

You’ll learn:

  • How to optimize your resume for software engineering roles.

  • What employers look for in your GitHub and LinkedIn profiles.

  • How to prepare for technical interviews.

Link to course: Getting a Software Engineering Job, v3 Frontend Masters

2. Create Your Job Search To-Do List

📝 After watching the course, create a Google Doc or Notion page titled:
“Software Engineering Job To-Do List”

Break it into the following categories, and list at least 2–3 actionable items under each. There are some given as examples:

  • Resume Optimization

    • Add relevant keywords for software engineering roles.

    • Quantify project results.

    • Include links to GitHub and LinkedIn.

  • GitHub Profile Enhancements

    • Upload key projects that show your skills.

    • Write clear documentation for each.

    • Refactor code and add helpful comments.

  • LinkedIn Profile Improvements

    • Update headline to reflect your career goals.

    • Add a strong summary highlighting your skills and value.

    • Request recommendations from peers or mentors.

  • Technical Interview Preparation

    • Practice algorithms and data structures.

    • Do coding challenges (LeetCode, HackerRank, etc.).

    • Review common problem-solving strategies.

  • Behavioral Interview Preparation

    • Research common behavioral interview questions.

    • Write out STAR (Situation, Task, Action, Result) stories for at least 3–5 past projects or experiences.

    • Practice answering questions aloud to build confidence.

  • Networking & Job Applications

    • Research target companies and recruiters.

    • Attend virtual job fairs or tech meetups.

✅ Example action items:
"Draft 3 STAR stories about team collaboration, problem-solving, and overcoming challenges."
"Polish GitHub project descriptions by explaining the tech stack and what problem each solves."

Once complete, share the link to your to-do list with Dawn at
📧 dawn@anniecannons.com

3. Upload Your Current Resume

📄 Upload your current resume, however rough, and yes, even if outdated.
No need to revise or update it yet. We will work on improvements together in a later assignment.
This gives us a starting point to build from.

4. Book a conversation with Dawn

📅 Schedule a one-on-one session with Dawn to review your plan and discuss next steps
👉 Reach out to Dawn on Slack direct message once you are ready to meet

📌 Deliverables

  1. Share the link to your Job Search To-Do List with Dawn at dawn@anniecannons.com.
  2. Upload your current resume (as-is).
  3. Reach out to Dawn and book a time to check in.

🔗 Resources

  • Getting a Software Engineering Job, v3 Frontend Masters

  • LeetCode

  • HackerRank

  5. Take a screenshot of this page, showing your speed, accuracy and current goal. 

 

How to turn this in

Take a screenshot of the page described in steps 6 and 7 above. Turn that in to this assignment.

If you need help taking a screenshot

This will describe how to do it: https://support.apple.com/en-us/102646Links to an external site. 

Alternatively, take a photo of your screen with your phone. Upload the screenshot to the assignment below.

 

Late assignments/ Incomplete Assignments

In the case of incomplete assignments, please note that it is crucial to provide as much information as possible. This will help us understand why you were unable to meet the deadline and how we can best support you in completing the work.

In the text entry for the assignment, please include the following:

  1. Why the assignment is late
  2. What you still need to do for the assignment
  3. Your expected timeline for completing the work
  4. If you would like to copy and paste the example here as a template please do so

For example:

 “The ________ project is not complete because _________. I have ________, ________, and ________ complete but still need more time to complete _________, _________, and _________. I estimate that I will have this done by _________________.”

assignments.title: Week 9 Update your to-do list
assignments.description_html:
What is a To-Do list?

Each week, you will update a To-Do list.

This list includes:

  • Assignments you have not yet turned in
  • Assignments you have turned in but have not been graded yet
  • Assignments that have been graded "incomplete"

Where to find your list

Your list is already created for you in your Notion Dashboard under the "To Do List" Section.

Update your list

As you get new assignments, add them to the list. It's suggested you take a few minutes every day at the end of Block D to update your list.

  • What assignments were you given today but you have not yet completed? Add these to the list.
  • What assignments have you finished and submitted? Check them off/remove them from your list.
  • Check the gradebook. Do you have assignments that are marked incomplete? Add these to your list.

If you update your list every day, it's easy to stay on top of your work. You'll also reduce stress about missing assignments or not knowing where to start.

When to attend Focus Friday

You are always welcome to attend Focus Friday. We love to see ITP students working on code with our more advanced students. 

You must attend Focus Friday if you have items remaining on your to-do list at the end of Thursday.This will give you focus time to get those assignments completed. Instructors are on hand to help you as well.

How to turn in your to-do list

Each week, we check your list to make sure you're keeping it up to date. Turn in a link to your list via this assignment.

  1. Why the assignment is late
  2. What you still need to do for the assignment
  3. Your expected timeline for completing the work
  4. If you would like to copy and paste the example here as a template please do so

For example:

 “The ________ project is not complete because _________. I have ________, ________, and ________ complete but still need more time to complete _________, _________, and _________. I estimate that I will have this done by _________________.”
